questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi is noted as which kind of Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Rajiv Gandhi became the youngest Prime Minister of India at the age of 40.

questions.q: At what age did Rajiv Gandhi become Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He became Prime Minister at the age of 40 in 1984.

questions.q: In which year did Rajiv Gandhi become Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Rajiv Gandhi became Prime Minister in 1984.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi served as Prime Minister until which year?
questions.explanation: He served as Prime Minister from 1984 until 1989.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi is associated with initiatives to modernise which sectors in India?
questions.explanation: He is associated with initiatives to modernise telecommunications and computing.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi belonged to which prominent political family of India?
questions.explanation: Rajiv Gandhi belonged to the Nehru-Gandhi family; his mother was Indira Gandhi.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi's premiership is closely linked with the early push for which technology in India?
questions.explanation: His tenure is linked with an early push to spread computers and information technology.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi became Prime Minister immediately following the death in office of which leader?
questions.explanation: He became Prime Minister in 1984 following the death of Indira Gandhi.

questions.q: Indira Gandhi, who preceded Rajiv Gandhi as Prime Minister, was his what?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was Rajiv Gandhi's mother.

questions.q: For how many years did Rajiv Gandhi serve as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He served roughly five years, from 1984 to 1989.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi is often described as which sequential Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Rajiv Gandhi is counted as the sixth Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi's record as youngest Prime Minister relates to his age at assuming office, which was younger than that of which earlier holder?
questions.explanation: At 40 he was younger at assuming office than every previous Prime Minister, making him the youngest.

questions.q: Which constitutional amendment, associated with Rajiv Gandhi's tenure, lowered the voting age from 21 to 18?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment of 1988, during his tenure, lowered the voting age from 21 to 18.

questions.q: The Anti-Defection Law, added through the Tenth Schedule, was enacted by the 52nd Amendment during which Prime Minister's tenure?
questions.explanation: The Anti-Defection Law was introduced by the 52nd Amendment in 1985, during Rajiv Gandhi's tenure.

questions.q: Initiatives to spread computing and telecommunications in the mid-1980s are most associated with which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Mid-1980s initiatives to spread computing and telecommunications are most associated with Rajiv Gandhi.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi's tenure as Prime Minister fell entirely within which decade?
questions.explanation: His tenure from 1984 to 1989 fell entirely within the 1980s.

questions.q: Which constitutional amendments giving local self-government bodies a stronger framework were shaped by ideas Rajiv Gandhi championed and enacted shortly after his tenure?
questions.explanation: The 73rd and 74th Amendments on local self-government drew on ideas Rajiv Gandhi championed and were enacted in 1992, shortly after his tenure.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi's tenure as Prime Minister concluded after which event of 1989?
questions.explanation: His tenure concluded following the general election of 1989.

questions.q: Which Prime Minister succeeded Rajiv Gandhi in office in 1989?
questions.explanation: V.P. Singh succeeded Rajiv Gandhi as Prime Minister in 1989.

questions.q: Lowering the national voting age to 18 expanded the electorate by enfranchising which group?
questions.explanation: The reform during his tenure enfranchised citizens aged 18 to 20 who could not previously vote.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi's premiership is often cited as marking an early policy emphasis on which broad theme?
questions.explanation: His premiership is cited for an early emphasis on technological modernisation.

questions.q: Within the Nehru-Gandhi line of Prime Ministers, Rajiv Gandhi followed which two predecessors from his own family?
options: Lal Bahadur Shastri and Morarji Desai
questions.explanation: He followed his grandfather Jawaharlal Nehru and his mother Indira Gandhi as Prime Minister.

questions.q: The 61st Amendment, enacted during Rajiv Gandhi's tenure, amended which article dealing with the qualification for voting?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment amended Article 326 to lower the voting age from 21 to 18.

questions.q: The Anti-Defection Law enacted in 1985 added which Schedule to the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 52nd Amendment of 1985 added the Tenth Schedule, the Anti-Defection provisions, during his tenure.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi's status as youngest Prime Minister is measured against which benchmark?
options: His age at first taking the office
His age at the time of an election win only
questions.explanation: The youngest-Prime-Minister record is measured by his age, 40, when he first took the office.

questions.q: The amendment lowering the voting age to 18 during Rajiv Gandhi's tenure is officially titled the Constitution (Sixty-first Amendment) Act of which year?
questions.explanation: It is officially the Constitution (Sixty-first Amendment) Act, 1988; it received presidential assent and came into force in March 1989.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi entered the office of Prime Minister under which constitutional process upon the office falling vacant in 1984?
options: Direct popular election to the office
questions.explanation: Like all Prime Ministers, he was appointed by the President when the office fell vacant in 1984.

questions.q: Which pair of constitutional amendments on grassroots democracy, enacted in 1992, is associated with ideas Rajiv Gandhi promoted?
questions.explanation: The 73rd and 74th Amendments of 1992 on panchayats and urban bodies are associated with ideas he promoted.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi's tenure as Prime Minister directly preceded which broader phase in Indian national politics?
options: The era of coalition governments at the Union
questions.explanation: His 1984-1989 tenure directly preceded the era of coalition governments that began at the Union in 1989.

questions.q: Rajiv Gandhi is the third member of the Nehru-Gandhi family to have held which office?
questions.explanation: He was the third member of his family, after Nehru and Indira Gandhi, to serve as Prime Minister.
